summary: Updated the repository’s dtk-template-derived tooling and configuration: bumped decomp-toolkit, m2c, binutils, compilers, objdiff, and sjiswrap versions; added precompiled-header support to the project generator; expanded context-generation controls; added documentation for splits.txt and symbols.txt; and adjusted matching status for several item objects under configure.py. The PR was primarily infrastructure/tooling work rather than a symbol-level decomp PR.
what_changed: Bumped decomp-toolkit from 1.6.2 to 1.8.3 in both .nix/decomp-toolkit.nix and configure.py.
Bumped configured tool tags: binutils 2.42-1 to 2.42-2, compilers 20250520 to 20251118, objdiff v3.0.0 to v3.6.1, and sjiswrap v1.2.1 to v1.2.2; wibo remained pinned at 0.7.0.

Updated the Nix m2c source revision from f201e8861ba0143676f3078d7354d2f7255555b3 to 900b0b6a55307f6cd1270023f641c7380083d8c7 and adjusted the graphviz dependency removal patch to match the newer pyproject format.
Added ProjectConfig.precompiled_headers and PrecompiledHeader support in tools/project.py, including mwcc_pch and mwcc_pch_sjis Ninja rules that compile .pch/.pch++ headers to .mch files.
Added get_pch_out_name() using an absolute output path for .mch files so depfile paths from MWCC can be matched to the Ninja target and precompiled headers rebuild when changed.
Expanded file/language recognition and objdiff watch patterns to include .cc, .cxx, .c++, .hh, .hp, .hxx, .h++, .pch, and .pch++.
Added .vscode file associations mapping *.pch to C and *.pch++ to C++.
Added context-generation controls: ProjectConfig.context_exclude_globs and ProjectConfig.context_defines, decompctx.py -x/--exclude include-glob skipping, and -D/--define generated prelude macros.
Changed the decompctx Ninja rule to pass $excludes and $defines through to tools/decompctx.py.
Added docs/splits.md describing splits.txt sections, files, section attributes, comment overrides, order hints, common BSS, rename, skip, and REL vaddr behavior.
Added docs/symbols.md describing symbols.txt syntax and attributes such as type, size, scope, align, data, hidden, force_active, noreloc, noexport, and stripped.

Adjusted configure.py object status for multiple item files from Matching to NonMatching, including itheart.c, ittomato.c, itbox.c, itharisen.c, itlipstick.c, itmariocape.c, itchicorita.c, itthunder.c, and itfushigibana.c, and added itchicoritaleaf.c as NonMatching.
Expanded wibo wrapper eligibility in tools/project.py from Linux x86/i386 only to Linux or Darwin on i386, x86_64, aarch64, or arm64.
Changed wrapper implicit dependency handling so an existing wrapper or a downloaded wibo wrapper can become an implicit dependency.
Made small formatting/style updates across Python tools, including tools/decomp.py, tools/dep_graph.py, tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py, tools/split_suggester.py, and tools/wiki_tu.py.
Changed asm-differ ELF magic literal casing from b"\x7FELF" to b"\x7fELF", which is byte-equivalent and appears stylistic.
smart_moves: Kept tool version bumps centralized in configure.py and mirrored the decomp-toolkit version in the Nix package definition, reducing drift between local and Nix workflows.
Implemented precompiled header output naming as an absolute .mch path specifically to make MWCC depfile entries line up with Ninja targets, avoiding stale PCH rebuild bugs.
Made PCH compilation respect Shift-JIS wrapping through separate mwcc_pch and mwcc_pch_sjis rules, matching the existing source compilation split.
Inserted -lang=c or -lang=c++ automatically for PCH cflags when no -lang flag is already present, using filename classification to avoid requiring every PCH entry to repeat language flags.
Extended decompctx with include exclusion globs and prelude defines rather than hardcoding project-specific header workarounds, making the context generator reusable for difficult translation units.

Documented dtk file formats in-repo at the same time as updating dtk-template, which helps future contributors understand split and symbol metadata without relying only on external template knowledge.
Left wibo pinned at 0.7.0 after discussion, suggesting the PR avoided unnecessary tool churn when the current version was sufficient.
decomp_lessons: When updating dtk-template/toolchain versions, expect matching status to be revisited; this PR demoted several item objects from Matching to NonMatching, which is evidence that template/tool changes can expose or change match behavior.

PCH support in decomp build systems needs depfile-aware target naming. If MWCC emits absolute or filesystem paths for .mch dependencies, Ninja targets must match those paths or rebuilds can silently fail.
Context-generation tools benefit from explicit macro prelude support. Use -D-style defines to reproduce compile-time conditions when generating m2c/decomp contexts.

Context-generation tools also benefit from include exclusion globs for problematic or irrelevant headers, while preserving breadcrumbs in the generated context via comments such as skipped excluded file.
For dtk splits, file paths in splits.txt correspond to configure.py unit entries for compiler flags/options, and the source file does not need to exist before starting a split.
Use splits.txt order: only for ambiguous link-order fine tuning; the docs added here state decomp-toolkit normally resolves link order automatically.

Use symbols.txt noreloc for data that resembles pointers but must not be interpreted as addresses, and force_active/noexport/stripped for linker-export, code-merging, and common-BSS edge cases respectively.
For assembly-only files that were not compiled with mwcc, splits.txt comment:0 can disable .comment generation; the new docs note that generating a .comment section for such files can crash mwld.
naming_conventions: Precompiled header sources use .pch for C and .pch++ for C++.
Precompiled header outputs are generated as .mch files under the build output include tree.
tools/project.py introduced a PrecompiledHeader dictionary type alias and ProjectConfig.precompiled_headers list.
Context-control ProjectConfig fields are named context_exclude_globs and context_defines.
decompctx command-line options use -x/--exclude for excluded include filename globs and -D/--define for prelude macro definitions.
configure.py continues to express per-object status with Object(Matching, "path") and Object(NonMatching, "path").
splits.txt uses section/file attributes such as type:, align:, vaddr:, comment:, order:, start:, end:, rename:, common, and skip.
symbols.txt uses symbol attributes such as type:, size:, scope:, align:, data:, hidden, force_active, noreloc, noexport, and stripped.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: After the template/tool update, several item translation units were explicitly marked NonMatching, preserving build correctness rather than pretending they still matched under the new configuration.

The docs added here encode common matching tactics for dtk projects: use order: to break ambiguous object ordering, comment:0 for non-mwcc assembly objects, common for common BSS, skip for linker-generated data, and rename: for generated section names like .ctors$10.
tools/project.py retains extab-clean post-processing via dtk extab clean with extab_padding for MWCC objects that need exception-table cleanup.
tools/decomp.py still invokes m2c with ppc-mwcc-c target context and options such as --knr and --pointer left; the diff mostly reformatted these arguments rather than introducing a new tactic.
decompctx.py now supports excluding selected includes and injecting defines, which is useful when constructing smaller, more accurate decompilation contexts for m2c or manual matching.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the PR should use r-burns's patch of wibo instead.
review_feedback.evidence: Issue comment: "@r-burns What about just using your patch of wibo?"

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Said using the patch would work too, but the current approach seemed easier; also questioned whether recent wibo releases were needed because they had been happily pinned to 0.7.0.
review_feedback.evidence: Issue comment: "That works too, this seemed easier. Do we actually need anything from recent releases of wibo? I've been happily pinned to 0.7.0."

follow_up_queries: Which item files demoted to NonMatching in PR 2203 have since been restored to Matching?
How is ProjectConfig.precompiled_headers configured in later PRs or downstream dtk-template updates?
Do later PRs update wibo beyond 0.7.0 or adopt r-burns's wibo patch?
Which translation units use decompctx context_exclude_globs or context_defines after this PR?
Did the decomp-toolkit 1.8.3 update require changes to splits.txt or symbols.txt in later PRs?
